nodes.description: Eye movement abnormalities including rotary nystagmus, exotropia, and strabismus.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: the common presenting features include strabismus (N = 11/13, 85%), epilepsy (N = 10/13, 77%)

nodes.description: Reduced ventilation due to impaired central respiratory control, with blunted responses to hypoxia and hypercapnia. May require BiPAP support.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: the common presenting features include strabismus (N = 11/13, 85%), epilepsy (N = 10/13, 77%), and central or obstructive sleep apnea

nodes.description: Loss of P4H-TM leads to impaired central respiratory control, with hypoventilation and blunted responses to hypoxia. Patients may require BiPAP support and are at risk for respiratory distress during infections.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Hypoventilation, obstructive and central sleep apnea, and dysautonomia were identified as novel features associated with the phenotype.

nodes.description: Some patients develop coarse facial features.
Autonomic dysfunction including temperature dysregulation and constipation. Some patients show ROHHAD-like features including rapid weight gain.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Some patients also show dysautonomia, including constipation (N = 6/13, 46%), recurrent hypothermia or hyperthermia (N = 4/13, 31%), and reduced sweating (N = 2/13, 15%)

nodes.description: P4H-TM is a regulator of calcium dynamics and gliotransmission in astrocytes via a HIF-1-dependent mechanism. Loss of P4H-TM impairs receptor-operated and store-operated calcium entry (ROCE/SOCE), reduces mitochondrial calcium uptake and ATP production, and attenuates gliotransmission.
Seizures in most patients, often beginning in infancy, with EEG showing multifocal spikes. Multiple seizure types may occur but are often relatively well-controlled with anticonvulsants.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: the common presenting features include strabismus (N = 11/13, 85%), epilepsy (N = 10/13, 77%), and central or obstructive sleep apnea

nodes.description: Delayed walking or inability to walk, with unsteady wide-based gait in those who achieve ambulation.
Decreased muscle tone present from infancy, contributing to motor delays.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: All the patients have hypotonia (N = 13/13), and either intellectual disability (N = 12/13) or developmental delay

nodes.description: Significant delays in motor, speech, and cognitive development beginning in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: All the patients have hypotonia (N = 13/13), and either intellectual disability (N = 12/13) or developmental delay

nodes.description: Cognitive impairment ranging from severe to profound, often with absent or severely limited speech.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: All the patients have hypotonia (N = 13/13), and either intellectual disability (N = 12/13) or developmental delay

nodes.description: Tendency toward obesity, sometimes with rapid weight gain similar to ROHHAD syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Six patients (N = 6/13, 46%) have a body mass index (BMI) over 25 kg/m2

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Variant characterization demonstrates that the variants affect protein folding by yielding an insoluble protein product.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characterization, genetic mapping and whole-genome sequence analysis of a novel autosomal recessive intellectual disability syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Whole-genome sequencing of one affected individual pinpointed three genes with potentially protein damaging homozygous sequence changes within the predisposition locus transketolase (TKT), prolyl 4-hydroxylase transmembrane (P4HTM), and ubiquitin specific peptidase 4 (USP4).

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in P4HTM result in loss of ER-resident transmembrane prolyl 4-hydroxylase activity. The enzyme contains an EF-hand domain that allows calcium-dependent regulation of its catalytic activity, linking the genetic defect to both HIF signaling and calcium homeostasis pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA).

nodes.description: Recurrent pneumonia and respiratory distress, likely related to hypoventilation and aspiration risk.
Spinal curvature abnormality developing in some patients.
Visual problems including myopia, hyperopia, astigmatism, optic atrophy, cortical blindness, or achromic fundi.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic loss-of-function P4HTM gene variants cause hypotonia, hypoventilation, intellectual disability, dysautonomia, epilepsy, and eye abnormalities (HIDEA syndrome).
evidence.snippet: All the patients with P4HTM gene variants have developmental delay or intellectual disability and visual abnormalities—such as strabismus, rotating eye movements, nystagmus, or cortical blindness
